sources.name: RBI Handbook of Statistics on Indian Economy 2024-25 - Table 10: Per Capita Net State Domestic Product - State-wise (At Constant Prices)
sources.authority: Reserve Bank of India (compiled from National Statistics Office, MoSPI)

sources.name: RBI Handbook of Statistics on Indian Economy - landing page

license.name: Reserve Bank of India publication (open for non-commercial use with attribution)

indicator.title: State per-capita NSDP (constant prices, long series, spliced)
indicator.description: Real per-capita NSDP, 2000-01 -> 2024-25, spliced across MoSPI's 1999-2000, 2004-05, and 2011-12 base years. The most recent base is kept for overlapping years; each row's `vintage` records which base produced the value. Long-history sibling of `economy/state_per_capita_nsdp_constant_2011_12_inr` (which is single-base 2011-12 only).

denominator.note: Per-capita = NSDP ÷ state mid-year population estimate

indicator.notes: Source: RBI Handbook of Statistics on Indian Economy 2024-25 edition, Table 10. Pre-2011-12 figures are real per-capita NSDP at factor cost (older bases); 2011-12 onwards are at basic prices. Cross-base growth rates not strictly comparable.
indicator.methodology_vintage: MoSPI multi-base spliced (1993-94 / 1999-2000 / 2004-05 / 2011-12); RBI Handbook 2024-25 edition

series_breaks.note: MoSPI rebase to 2011-12 base AND switched headline aggregate from 'NSDP at factor cost' (pre-2011-12) to 'NSDP at basic prices' (2011-12 onwards). Growth rates spanning 2010-11 -> 2011-12 are not strictly comparable.

Telangana carved out of Andhra Pradesh on 2 June 2014. RBI back-projects S29 (Telangana) to 2011-12 by carving from undivided AP; pre-2014-15 S29 values are MoSPI back-estimates, not original state DES figures. S01 (residual Andhra Pradesh) is reported on the post-bifurcation footprint from 2011-12 too.
J&K reorganisation (Aug 2019): U08 series from 2019-20 onwards covers UT of Jammu & Kashmir only (Ladakh not separately reported in this RBI table).

series_spec.description: Real per-capita NSDP, 2000-01 -> 2024-25, spliced across MoSPI's 1999-2000, 2004-05, and 2011-12 base years. The most recent base is kept for overlapping years; each row's `vintage` records which base produced the value. Long-history sibling of `economy/state_per_capita_nsdp_constant_2011_12_inr` (which is single-base 2011-12 only).

The most recent base is kept for overlapping years; each row's `vintage` records which base produced the value.
